passages.title: A Lost Wallet
passages.text: Last Tuesday, Sujin was walking home from the library. She was thinking about her homework when she saw a brown leather wallet on the sidewalk. She picked it up and opened it. Inside, there was some money and a driver's license. Sujin realized that she should find the owner. She felt a little bit of panic, not knowing what to do. Then, she saw a police station across the street. She went inside and gave the wallet to a police officer. The next day, a man called her. He was the owner of the wallet. He was very thankful and called her an honest student. He even sent a small reward to her school. Sujin felt proud of doing the right thing.

questions.text: 위 글의 주제로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: A visit to the police station
An experience of finding a lost wallet
How to get a driver's license
questions.explanation: 잃어버린 지갑을 발견하고 주인에게 돌려준 경험이 글의 중심 내용

questions.text: What did Sujin do right after she found the wallet?
choices: She went to the owner's house.
She took it to the police station.
questions.explanation: 당황했지만 길 건너편 경찰서에 가서 지갑을 주었다고 나옴

questions.text: 위 글의 내용과 일치하지 않는 것은?
choices: 지갑 안에는 돈과 신분증이 들어 있었다.
수진이는 옳은 일을 한 자신을 자랑스러워했다.
questions.explanation: 지갑 주인이 학교로 보상을 보냈다고 했으므로, 경찰관이 준 것이 아님

passages.title: Mike's First Cooking Class
passages.text: Mike loves eating pizza, but he has never made one. Last weekend, he joined a kids' cooking class. The chef first showed everyone how to make the dough. Mike followed the recipe carefully. He mixed flour, water, and yeast. It was a bit messy, and he got flour all over his apron. Next, he added tomato sauce, cheese, and his favorite toppings: pepperoni and mushrooms. He put his pizza into the big oven. While waiting, he was very nervous. Finally, his pizza was ready. It looked and smelled wonderful. He took a big bite. It was the most delicious pizza he had ever tasted! He couldn't wait to make one for his family.

questions.text: 위 글의 제목으로 가장 적절한 것은?
questions.explanation: 마이크가 요리 수업에 참여해 피자를 만든 즐거운 경험을 다룸

questions.text: 위 글의 내용과 일치하는 것을 고르시오.
choices: 마이크는 피자를 오븐에 넣는 것을 잊었다.
마이크는 자신이 만든 피자가 아주 맛있다고 생각했다.
요리사가 마이크의 피자를 대신 만들어 주었다.
questions.explanation: 자신이 만든 피자를 먹고 "most delicious"라고 생각했음

questions.text: According to the passage, which topping did Mike NOT use?
questions.explanation: 토마토소스, 치즈, 페퍼로니, 버섯은 언급되었지만 올리브는 언급되지 않음

passages.title: My Pet Hamster, Choco
passages.text: I have a pet hamster named Choco. He is a small, round, and furry creature. He lives in a big cage with a wheel, a water bottle, and a small house. During the day, Choco mostly sleeps inside his house. But when the night comes, he becomes very active. He runs on his wheel for hours. Sometimes the sound of the wheel wakes me up, but I don't mind. I love to observe him. I am responsible for feeding him and cleaning his cage every week. Taking care of a pet is a lot of work, but it is also very rewarding. Choco is a small animal, but he is a big part of my life.

questions.text: What is this passage mainly about?
choices: The writer's experience with a pet
The difficulties of cleaning a cage
How to buy a healthy hamster
Animals that are active at night
questions.explanation: 글쓴이가 애완 햄스터 초코를 키우는 경험에 대해 이야기하고 있음

questions.text: According to the passage, when is Choco most active?
choices: When the writer is at school
questions.explanation: 본문에 "when the night comes, he becomes very active"라고 명시됨

questions.text: 위 글의 내용으로 알 수 없는 것은?
choices: 글쓴이는 애완동물을 돌보는 것에 보람을 느낀다.
questions.explanation: 햄스터를 언제 샀는지에 대한 정보는 글에 나와 있지 않음

passages.title: Volunteering at a Nursing Home
passages.text: Last Saturday, my friend Jina and I did some volunteer work. We went to a local nursing home. A nursing home is a place where elderly people who need care live. At first, I was a little nervous because I had never been to one before. Our job was to read books to the residents and play board games with them. I was paired with an old woman named Mrs. Park. She couldn't see well, so I read a newspaper for her. She listened carefully and smiled. Later, Jina and I helped serve lunch. All the elderly people were very kind to us. It was a valuable experience. I learned that spending time with others is a great way to show love.

questions.text: What is the main purpose of the writer?
choices: To ask for donations for a nursing home
To complain about a boring weekend
To give information about board games
To explain how to read to the elderly
questions.explanation: 요양원에서 자원봉사를 한 경험을 시간 순서에 따라 묘사하고 있음

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT mentioned as an activity the writer did?
questions.explanation: 신문 읽기, 보드게임, 점심 배식은 언급되었지만 방 청소는 언급되지 않음

questions.text: 글쓴이가 처음에 요양원에서 느꼈을 심경으로 가장 적절한 것은?
questions.explanation: 본문에 "I was a little nervous"라고 직접적으로 언급됨

passages.title: The First Snow
passages.text: This morning when I woke up, I saw something amazing outside my window. The world was white! Big, soft flakes of snow were falling from the sky. It was the first snow of the year. The snow covered everything—the trees, the roofs, and the cars. It looked like the world was covered in a soft, white blanket. The whole town was quiet and peaceful. I quickly put on my warmest clothes and ran outside. The air was cold and fresh. I made a snowball and threw it at a tree. The atmosphere was so magical. I went back inside and made a cup of hot chocolate. Drinking it by the window and watching the snow fall made me feel very cozy.

questions.text: 위 글의 제목으로 가장 적절한 것은?
questions.explanation: 첫눈이 내린 날의 아름다운 풍경과 글쓴이의 감상을 다루고 있음

questions.text: 위 글의 내용과 일치하지 않는 것은?
choices: 글쓴이는 창밖을 보고 눈이 온 것을 알았다.
눈은 모든 것을 하얀 담요처럼 덮었다.
questions.explanation: 눈덩이를 만들어 나무에 던졌다고 했지, 다른 사람과 눈싸움을 했다는 내용은 없음

questions.text: 밑줄 친 cozy의 의미로 가장 적절한 것은?
questions.explanation: 따뜻한 코코아를 마시며 눈을 보는 상황에서 느끼는 '아늑하고 편안한' 감정을 의미

passages.title: School Sports Day
passages.text: Our school's annual sports day was held yesterday. The sky was clear and everyone was full of energy. All students were divided into two teams: the Blue Team and the White Team. I was on the Blue Team. I decided to compete in the 100-meter race and the relay race. I didn't win the 100-meter race, but I tried my best. The most exciting event was the relay race. I was the last runner for my team. When I got the baton, the White Team was slightly ahead. I ran as fast as I could. My friends and teachers cheered for me loudly. In the end, I passed the other runner and we won! I was so tired, but incredibly happy. Sports day is not just about winning; it's about teamwork and effort.

questions.text: What is the main idea of this passage?
choices: The writer's exciting experience on sports day
How to win a relay race
The importance of being on the winning team
questions.explanation: 운동회 날 겪은 여러 가지 일과 느낌을 중심으로 서술하고 있음

questions.text: Which event did the writer's team win?
questions.explanation: 100미터 경주에서는 졌지만, 이어달리기에서는 이겼다고 나옴

questions.text: 위 글의 내용과 일치하는 것을 고르시오.
choices: 글쓴이는 운동회가 끝난 후 매우 슬펐다.
questions.explanation: 본문에 "I was the last runner for my team"이라고 명시됨

passages.title: A Mysterious Noise
passages.text: One night, I was trying to sleep when I heard a strange noise. It was a soft creak, and it seemed to come from downstairs. At first, I tried to ignore it, but I heard it again. My heart started beating faster. Was it a thief? I decided to be brave and investigate. I quietly got out of bed and tiptoed down the stairs. The noise was getting louder. It was coming from the kitchen. I peeked around the corner and saw a small shadow moving. I was so scared! But then, I saw what it was. It was just my new puppy, Max, trying to get a cookie from the jar on the counter. I was so relieved. I picked him up and said, "Max, you scared me!" The source of the mystery was my cute puppy.

questions.text: What is the best title for this story?
questions.explanation: 밤에 부엌에서 들려온 정체불명의 소리가 글의 핵심 사건임

questions.text: How did the writer feel when he first heard the noise?
questions.explanation: 본문에 "My heart started beating faster", "I was so scared" 등의 표현이 나옴

questions.text: What was the real source of the strange noise?
questions.explanation: 소리의 정체는 카운터 위 과자를 꺼내려던 강아지였음

passages.title: Our Small Balcony Garden
passages.text: My family lives in an apartment, so we don't have a big yard. But we have a small balcony, and my mom decided to start a small gardening project there. First, we bought some large pots, good soil, and a few seed packets. We chose to plant cherry tomatoes, lettuce, and some herbs like basil. My job was to fill the pots with soil and plant the seeds. It was fun to play with the dirt. Every day, I watered the plants and checked on them. For about a week, nothing happened. I was losing patience. But then, one morning, I saw a tiny green sprout coming out of the soil! I was so happy. Gardening teaches me that good things take time.

questions.text: What is the main purpose of this passage?
choices: To explain the benefits of eating vegetables
To describe the experience of starting a small garden
To compare apartment life and house life
To sell gardening tools and seeds
To complain about living in an apartment
questions.explanation: 가족이 아파트 발코니에서 작은 정원을 가꾸기 시작한 경험을 설명함

questions.text: According to the passage, which of the following did the family NOT plant?
questions.explanation: 체리 토마토, 상추, 허브(바질)는 심었지만 당근은 언급되지 않음

questions.text: 밑줄 친 patience의 의미로 가장 적절한 것은?
questions.explanation: 싹이 나기까지 기다리는 상황에서 필요한 '차분하게 기다리는 능력'을 의미

passages.title: A Day at the Dinosaur Museum
passages.text: Last weekend, my dad took me to the Natural History Museum. My favorite part of the museum is the dinosaur exhibit. As soon as we entered the hall, I saw a huge Tyrannosaurus Rex skeleton. It was so big that I felt like a tiny ant next to it. The sign said the T-Rex was a powerful hunter. I also saw the skeleton of a Triceratops, with its three big horns. There were many other fossils of ancient plants and sea creatures. I spent a long time looking at all the exhibits and reading the explanations. It's amazing to think that these giant creatures walked the Earth millions of years ago. It was a fascinating day.

questions.text: Where did the writer go last weekend?
questions.explanation: 글의 첫 문장에 "Natural History Museum"에 갔다고 명시됨

questions.text: What impressed the writer the most at the museum?
questions.explanation: 본문에 "huge Tyrannosaurus Rex skeleton"을 보고 매우 감명받았다고 서술됨

questions.text: 위 글의 내용과 일치하지 않는 것은?
choices: 트리케라톱스는 뿔이 세 개 달려 있었다.
questions.explanation: 글의 마지막에 "It was a fascinating day"라고 하며 매우 흥미로운 하루였다고 했으므로, 지루했다는 것은 틀린 설명

passages.title: The Power of Sleep
passages.text: Why is sleep so important? Many students stay up late to study or play games, but not getting enough sleep is a bad idea. Sleep is essential for both your body and your brain. While you are sleeping, your body repairs itself and your brain organizes the information from the day. This helps you remember what you learned. If you don't get enough sleep, you will feel tired and won't be able to concentrate in class. It can also affect your mood, making you feel easily upset. Doctors say teenagers need about 8 to 10 hours of sleep every night. So, make sure to turn off your phone, go to bed early, and let your brain recharge!

questions.text: What is the main topic of this passage?
choices: The reasons why sleep is important
The best way to study for a test
The effects of playing computer games
questions.explanation: 글 전체가 수면이 우리 몸과 뇌에 왜 중요한지를 여러 근거를 들어 설명하고 있음

questions.text: According to the passage, what is NOT a result of lacking sleep?
questions.explanation: 잠을 못 자면 집중을 못 해 학업에 방해가 되므로, 더 나은 학생이 된다는 것은 잠 부족의 결과가 아님

questions.text: 위 글에 따르면, 십 대 청소년에게 필요한 수면 시간은 얼마인가?
questions.explanation: 본문에 "teenagers need about 8 to 10 hours of sleep"라고 명시됨

passages.title: A Family History Project
passages.text: For my history class, I had a special assignment: to create a family tree. At first, I thought it would be boring. But I was wrong. I started by interviewing my grandparents. They told me amazing stories about their parents and even their grandparents. I learned that my great-grandfather was a teacher in a small village. My great-grandmother was famous for her beautiful singing. I discovered so many interesting facts about my ancestors. I collected old black-and-white photos and put them on a large poster board. My family tree wasn't just a list of names; it was a map of stories. This project helped me understand who I am and where I come from.

questions.text: What was the writer's history assignment about?
choices: Writing a report about a famous person
Making a map of a village
questions.explanation: 역사 숙제로 '가계도 만들기'를 했다는 내용이 글의 중심

questions.text: 위 글의 내용과 일치하는 것을 고르시오.
choices: 글쓴이는 처음에 숙제가 재미있을 것이라고 생각했다.
글쓴이는 숙제를 하면서 조상에 대해 많은 것을 알게 되었다.
글쓴이는 숙제를 위해 컬러 사진을 사용했다.
questions.explanation: 조부모님을 인터뷰하며 조상에 대한 흥미로운 사실들을 발견했다고 나옴

questions.text: How did the writer feel about the project in the end?
choices: It was a valuable and meaningful experience.
It was too difficult to finish.
questions.explanation: 글의 마지막에 "이 프로젝트는 내가 누구인지, 어디서 왔는지 이해하도록 도와주었다"고 하며 가치 있고 의미 있는 경험이었음을 알 수 있음

passages.title: My Stamp Collection
passages.text: My hobby is collecting stamps. It started when my grandfather gave me his old stamp album. The album was full of colorful stamps from all over the world. I was fascinated. Now, I have my own collection. Each stamp is like a small window to another country. Some stamps show famous people, while others show beautiful animals or historic buildings. My favorite stamp is a rare one from Brazil that shows a toucan. Whenever I get a new stamp, I use a magnifying glass to look at all the tiny details. Then, I carefully place it in my album. Collecting stamps is a fun way to learn about geography and culture.

questions.text: 위 글의 주제로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: How to travel around the world
The history of the postal service
questions.explanation: 우표 수집이 왜 재미있고 무엇을 배울 수 있는지에 대한 글

questions.text: Why does the writer like collecting stamps?
choices: It is an easy way to make money.
It helps him learn about different countries.
His friends all have the same hobby.
He wants to be a designer.
He likes using a magnifying glass.
questions.explanation: 본문에 "Collecting stamps is a fun way to learn about geography and culture"라고 명시됨

questions.text: 위 글의 내용과 일치하지 않는 것은?
choices: 글쓴이가 가장 좋아하는 우표는 브라질의 펭귄 우표이다.
글쓴이는 우표를 통해 지리와 문화를 배운다.
questions.explanation: 가장 좋아하는 우표는 브라질의 '투칸(toucan)' 우표라고 했으므로 펭귄 우표는 틀린 설명

passages.title: A Language Learning App
passages.text: I have always wanted to learn Japanese because I love anime. So, I downloaded a language learning app on my smartphone. The app is designed to make learning fun. It has many games and quizzes. For example, I can play a game to match Japanese words with pictures. The app also helps with my pronunciation. I can record my voice and compare it to a native speaker's. I try to use the app for at least 15 minutes every day. It's very convenient because I can use it on the bus or while waiting for a friend. Thanks to this app, I can now introduce myself and say simple greetings in Japanese. Technology is a great tool for learning new things.

questions.text: What is the main purpose of the passage?
choices: To explain how to use a smartphone
To share a fun experience of learning a language with an app
To complain about the difficulty of learning Japanese
To advertise a new language school
questions.explanation: 언어 학습 앱을 사용해 일본어를 재미있게 배우는 경험을 공유하고 있음

questions.text: According to the passage, which is NOT a feature of the app?
choices: Comparing the user's voice to a native speaker's
questions.explanation: 게임, 퀴즈, 발음 연습 기능은 언급되었지만, 애니메이션 영화 전체를 보는 기능은 언급되지 않음

questions.text: 위 글의 내용으로 보아, 글쓴이가 일본어를 배우는 주된 이유는?
questions.explanation: 글의 첫 문장에 "I have always wanted to learn Japanese because I love anime"라고 명시됨

passages.title: Grandma's Surprise Birthday Party
passages.text: My grandmother's 70th birthday was last Sunday. My family decided to throw her a surprise party. We all had different roles. My dad was in charge of inviting all our relatives. My mom and I baked a special sweet potato cake because it's my grandmother's favorite. My younger brother made a birthday card with lots of colorful drawings. On the day of the party, we all hid in the living room. When my grandmother walked in, we all shouted, "Surprise!" She was so shocked and happy that she started to cry a little. She said it was the best present she had ever received. Seeing her happy face made all the preparation worthwhile. It was a day full of love and joy.

questions.text: What did the family do for the grandmother's birthday?
choices: They took her to a restaurant.
They bought her an expensive gift.
They threw her a surprise party.
They went on a trip with her.
questions.explanation: 가족들이 할머니를 위해 '깜짝 파티(surprise party)'를 열어주었다고 나옴

questions.text: 위 글에서 글쓴이가 맡은 역할은 무엇이었는가?
questions.explanation: 본문에 "My mom and I baked a special sweet potato cake"라고 명시됨

questions.text: How did the grandmother react to the surprise?
choices: She was angry because she didn't know.
She was sad because some people didn't come.
She was shocked and very happy.
She was too tired to enjoy the party.
She pretended not to be surprised.
questions.explanation: 본문에 "She was so shocked and happy"라고 명시됨

passages.title: Planting a Tree for the Future
passages.text: On Arbor Day, my school held a special event. The goal was to make our school campus greener. Each class was given a small tree to plant. Our teacher explained why trees are so vital for our planet. They produce the oxygen we breathe, provide shade on hot days, and become homes for birds and insects. Our class received a small maple tree. We all worked together. Some students dug a hole, and others carefully placed the tree inside. I helped cover the roots with soil. Finally, we gave the tree plenty of water. Our teacher said, "This tree will grow with you." I felt a sense of accomplishment. I hope to see this tree grow tall and strong in the future.

questions.text: 위 글의 제목으로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: A Fun School Event: Planting a Tree
The Different Kinds of Maple Trees
My Favorite Day of the Year
questions.explanation: 식목일에 학교에서 나무를 심은 행사에 대한 글

questions.text: According to the passage, which is NOT a reason why trees are important?
questions.explanation: 산소 생산, 그늘 제공, 동물의 집 역할은 언급되었지만, 물을 깨끗하게 한다는 내용은 언급되지 않음

questions.text: 위 글의 내용과 일치하는 것을 고르시오.
choices: 각 학생은 나무를 한 그루씩 심었다.
글쓴이는 흙으로 나무뿌리를 덮는 것을 도왔다.
선생님은 나무가 빨리 자라지 않을 것이라고 말했다.
questions.explanation: 본문에 "I helped cover the roots with soil"이라고 명시됨

passages.title: Learning to Ride a Bike
passages.text: I still remember the day I learned how to ride a bike. I was seven years old, and my bike was bright blue. My dad took me to an empty park. He held the back of the bike while I pedaled. But every time he let go, I would lose my balance and fall. My knees got scraped, and I was scared. I wanted to give up. But my dad encouraged me. He said, "It's okay to fall. The important thing is to get back up." I tried again and again. Suddenly, I was pedaling on my own! I was actually riding the bike without any help. I felt like I was flying. It was a feeling of pure freedom and achievement. That day, I learned a valuable lesson: never give up.

questions.text: What is the main theme of this passage?
choices: A memory of learning to ride a bike
A father's love for his child
The best parks for bike riding
How to choose a good bike
questions.explanation: 어린 시절 자전거를 배우던 날의 기억을 상세히 묘사하고 있음

questions.text: How did the writer's father help him?
choices: He bought him a new bike.
He encouraged him not to give up.
He rode the bike for him.
questions.explanation: 아빠가 넘어져도 괜찮다며 계속 격려해 주었다고("encouraged me") 나옴

questions.text: 글쓴이가 자전거 타기에 성공했을 때 느낀 감정으로 가장 알맞은 것은?
questions.explanation: 본문에 "a feeling of pure freedom and achievement"라고 명시됨

passages.title: A Visit to the Aquarium
passages.text: Our class went on a field trip to the city aquarium last Friday. I was so excited because I love marine animals. The aquarium was huge and had many different sections. We saw scary sharks with sharp teeth and graceful rays that looked like they were flying in the water. My favorite part was the underwater tunnel. As we walked through it, fish of all colors and sizes swam all around us. It felt like I was walking on the ocean floor. We also watched a dolphin show. The dolphins were incredibly intelligent. They jumped high out of the water and did amazing tricks. The trip was not only fun but also educational. I learned a lot about the importance of protecting our oceans.

questions.text: Where did the writer go on a field trip?
questions.explanation: 글의 첫 문장과 제목에서 '수족관(aquarium)'에 갔음을 알 수 있음

questions.text: What was the writer's favorite part of the aquarium?
questions.explanation: 본문에 "My favorite part was the underwater tunnel"이라고 명시됨

questions.text: 위 글의 내용과 일치하지 않는 것은?
choices: 글쓴이는 바다를 보호할 필요가 없다고 생각했다.
수중 터널을 걸을 때 물고기들이 주변에서 헤엄쳤다.
questions.explanation: 글의 마지막에 "바다를 보호하는 것의 중요성에 대해 많이 배웠다"고 했으므로, 보호할 필요가 없다고 생각했다는 것은 틀린 설명

passages.title: A Friend in Need
passages.text: My best friend, Jiho, loves playing soccer. During a match last week, he fell badly and sprained his ankle. The doctor said he had to use crutches for two weeks and couldn't carry heavy things. Jiho was worried about school because his classroom is on the third floor and he has a lot of books. I decided to help him. Every morning, I went to his house to help him carry his bag to school. During breaks, I helped him get his lunch. Some of my other friends helped too. Jiho was very grateful. He said, "A friend in need is a friend indeed." This experience taught me that true friendship is not just about having fun together, but also about supporting each other in difficult times.

questions.text: What happened to Jiho last week?
choices: He got a perfect score on a test.
questions.explanation: 축구를 하다가 발목을 삐었다고("sprained his ankle") 나옴

questions.text: How did the writer help Jiho?
choices: He did Jiho's homework for him.
He carried Jiho's bag to school.
He taught Jiho how to play soccer.
He visited Jiho in the hospital every day.
questions.explanation: 학교 갈 때 지호의 가방을 들어주는 등 도움을 주었다고 나옴

questions.text: The phrase "A friend in need is a friend indeed" means:
choices: A true friend helps you when you have problems.
You should only be friends with people who need you.
It is important to have many friends.
You should not trust your friends.
questions.explanation: '어려울 때 친구가 진짜 친구다'라는 속담으로, 도움이 필요할 때 도와주는 친구가 진정한 친구라는 의미

passages.title: Making Kimchi with My Family
passages.text: In Korea, there is a tradition called "Kimjang" in late fall. It's when families get together to make large quantities of kimchi for the winter. Last weekend, my family did our Kimjang. My grandmother is the expert. She showed my mom and me how to properly salt the cabbage and mix the special sauce. The sauce is made with red pepper powder, garlic, ginger, and other ingredients. My job was to spread the sauce between the cabbage leaves. It was messy, but fun. We made enough kimchi to fill ten large jars. We shared some with our neighbors. Kimjang is more than just making food. It's a cultural heritage that brings family and community together.

questions.text: What is "Kimjang"?
choices: A Korean holiday in the spring
The tradition of making a lot of kimchi for winter
A special kind of spicy soup
The name of a famous Korean chef
questions.explanation: 늦가을에 겨울을 대비해 많은 양의 김치를 담그는 전통이라고 설명됨

questions.text: 위 글에 언급된 김치 양념 재료가 아닌 것은?
choices: 글쓴이의 역할은 양념을 배추 잎 사이에 바르는 것이었다.
questions.explanation: 고춧가루, 마늘, 생강은 언급되었지만 설탕은 언급되지 않음

questions.text: What does the writer think Kimjang is about?
choices: It is just a way to prepare food.
It is a difficult and boring chore.
It is a cultural heritage that connects people.
It is a competition to make the best kimchi.
It is a good way to sell kimchi.
questions.explanation: 글의 마지막에 "음식을 만드는 것 이상으로, 가족과 지역 사회를 하나로 묶는 문화유산"이라고 생각함

passages.title: The Most Important Meal of the Day
passages.text: Do you often skip breakfast because you are in a hurry? Many people do, but doctors say that breakfast is the most important meal of the day. It literally means to "break the fast" after a long night of sleep. Your brain and body need fuel to start the day. Eating a healthy breakfast helps you concentrate better at school and gives you the energy to be active. People who eat breakfast tend to have a healthier diet overall. If you skip breakfast, you might feel tired and hungry by mid-morning, and you may eat unhealthy snacks later. You don't need a big meal. A simple bowl of cereal, a piece of fruit, or some toast can make a big difference.

questions.text: What is the main idea of the passage?
choices: The best snacks to eat at school
How to lose weight by skipping meals
Different breakfast menus around the world
questions.explanation: 글 전체가 아침 식사를 왜 먹어야 하는지, 그 중요성에 대해 설명하고 있음

questions.text: According to the passage, what is a benefit of eating breakfast?
choices: It makes you feel more tired.
It makes you gain weight quickly.
It saves you time in the morning.
It makes you want to eat more snacks.
questions.explanation: 본문에 "Eating a healthy breakfast helps you concentrate better at school"이라고 명시됨

questions.text: 위 글의 내용과 일치하지 않는 것은?
choices: 아침 식사는 하루 중 가장 중요한 식사이다.
아침 식사를 거르면 학교에서 집중하기 어렵다.
아침 식사는 뇌와 몸에 에너지를 공급한다.
아침 식사는 항상 거창하게 먹어야 한다.
아침 식사를 거르면 건강에 좋지 않은 간식을 먹게 될 수 있다.
questions.explanation: 본문에 "You don't need a big meal"이라며 시리얼, 과일, 토스트 같은 간단한 식사도 괜찮다고 했으므로, 항상 거창하게 먹어야 한다는 것은 틀린 설명

passages.title: The School Flea Market
passages.text: Our school hosted a flea market last Saturday to raise money for a local charity. A flea market is a market where people sell used goods. My friends and I decided to participate. We gathered things we no longer used, like old books, toys, and clothes. We set up a small table in the school playground. At first, not many people visited our table. We were a bit disappointed. Then, my friend Minjun had a great idea. He made a colorful sign that said, "Great Items, Low Prices!" Soon, more students and parents started to come. We sold almost everything! In total, we earned 50,000 won. We felt proud to donate all the money to the charity. It was a fun way to help others and learn about economics.

questions.text: What was the purpose of the school flea market?
choices: To help students clean their rooms
To raise money for a charity
To give students a day off
To teach students how to make new things
To let students buy new school supplies
questions.explanation: 자선 단체를 위한 기금을 마련하기 위해("to raise money for a local charity") 벼룩시장을 열었다고 나옴

questions.text: What did the writer and his friends do to attract more customers?
choices: They lowered their prices to zero.
They moved their table to a different spot.
questions.explanation: 친구가 "colorful sign"을 만들어 더 많은 손님을 끌었다고 나옴

questions.text: 위 글의 내용과 일치하지 않는 것은?
choices: 글쓴이와 친구들은 낡은 책, 장난감 등을 팔았다.
처음부터 많은 사람들이 그들의 가게를 방문했다.
그들은 번 돈을 모두 자선 단체에 기부했다.
questions.explanation: 처음에는 사람들이 많이 오지 않아 실망했다고("a bit disappointed") 했으므로, 처음부터 많이 왔다는 것은 틀린 설명

passages.title: A Hike to the Summit
passages.text: Last weekend, my family and I went hiking on Mount Seorak. Our goal was to reach the Ulsanbawi Rock. The trail was steep and rocky. At the beginning, I was full of energy. But after an hour, my legs started to feel heavy, and I was out of breath. I wanted to turn back. My dad noticed I was struggling. He gave me some water and a piece of chocolate. He said, "Look how far we've come! The view from the top will be worth it." His words gave me new strength. We rested for a while and then continued our climb. Finally, we reached the summit. The view was breathtaking. I could see the entire city below me. I felt like I was on top of the world.

questions.text: What is the best title for this passage?
choices: How to Prepare for a Hike
The Different Trails of Mount Seorak
questions.explanation: 등산 과정의 어려움과 정상에 올랐을 때의 보람을 다루고 있으므로 '도전적이지만 보람 있는 등산'이 적절함

questions.text: How did the writer's father encourage him?
choices: He told him the view from the top would be great.
He promised to buy him a gift.
He said they could go home.
questions.explanation: 아빠가 정상에서의 경치가 그럴 만한 가치가 있을 것이라고("The view from the top will be worth it") 말하며 격려함

questions.text: 위 글에 나타난 글쓴이의 심경 변화로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: tired and discouraged → proud and happy
questions.explanation: 처음에는 힘들고 지쳐서 포기하고 싶었지만("tired and discouraged"), 정상에 오른 후에는 자랑스럽고 행복한("proud and happy") 감정을 느낌

passages.title: My First Time on a Plane
passages.text: When I was ten, my family went to Jeju Island for a vacation. It was my first time flying on an airplane. I was both excited and nervous. At the airport, I was amazed by the huge planes. When we boarded our plane, I found my window seat. I looked out the window as the plane started to move faster and faster. Then, I felt a gentle push as we took off. The houses and cars below us got smaller and smaller. Soon, we were flying above the clouds. It looked like a sea of white cotton. The flight attendant gave us juice and snacks. The one-hour flight felt very short. Landing was a bit bumpy, but it was exciting. It was an unforgettable experience.

questions.text: What is this passage mainly about?
choices: The writer's family vacation on Jeju Island
The writer's experience of flying for the first time
The job of a flight attendant
questions.explanation: 제주도 여행을 배경으로, 비행기를 처음 타 본 경험을 상세히 묘사하고 있음

questions.text: What did the writer see from the window after taking off?
choices: The city getting smaller and the clouds below
questions.explanation: 이륙 후 집과 차가 작아지고, 곧 구름 위를 날았다고("flying above the clouds") 나옴

questions.text: 위 글의 내용과 일치하는 것을 고르시오.
questions.explanation: 본문에 "we were flying above the clouds"라고 명시됨

passages.title: The Magic of Reading
passages.text: I used to think reading was boring. I preferred playing video games or watching TV. One rainy afternoon, my mom gave me a book called "The Lion, the Witch and the Wardrobe." She said it was her favorite book when she was a child. I had nothing else to do, so I started reading. The book was about four children who discover a magical land called Narnia. I was completely captivated. I forgot about the rain and the time. I felt like I was right there with the characters, having an adventure. Since that day, reading has become my favorite hobby. Books can take you to different worlds and let you live a thousand lives. It's a special kind of magic.

questions.text: What did the writer use to think about reading?
choices: It was better than video games.
questions.explanation: 글의 첫 문장에 "I used to think reading was boring"이라고 명시됨

questions.text: What made the writer change his mind about reading?
choices: He read a magical story about Narnia.
His friends told him to read more.
He won a book in a contest.
He wanted to learn new words.
questions.explanation: 엄마가 준 '나니아 연대기'라는 마법 같은 이야기를 읽고 독서에 대한 생각이 바뀜

questions.text: 위 글을 통해 알 수 있는 것은?
choices: '나니아 연대기'는 네 명의 아이들에 대한 이야기이다.
글쓴이는 책을 읽은 후 다시는 비디오 게임을 하지 않았다.
글쓴이는 책을 다 읽는 데 일주일이 걸렸다.
questions.explanation: 본문에 "The book was about four children who discover a magical land"라고 명시됨

passages.title: A City Mouse and a Country Mouse
passages.text: Once, a city mouse visited his cousin in the country. The country mouse offered him simple food like beans and corn. The city mouse didn't like it. "My dear cousin," he said, "your life is so poor. Come with me to the city and I will show you the good life." So, the country mouse went to the city. The city mouse lived in a big house. They went to the dining room. The table was full of delicious food like cake and cheese. But as they started to eat, a big cat jumped in. The mice ran for their lives. Later, two big dogs came in, barking loudly. The country mouse was terrified. "Goodbye, cousin," he said. "I would rather eat my simple food in peace than have your fine food in fear."

questions.text: 위 글의 장르로 가장 적절한 것은?
questions.explanation: 동물을 의인화하여 교훈을 주는 이야기이므로 '우화'에 해당함

questions.text: Why did the country mouse decide to go back to the country?
choices: He did not like the city food.
He was scared of the dangers in the city.
The city mouse was not kind to him.
questions.explanation: 고양이와 개들 때문에 겁에 질려서("terrified") 시골로 돌아가기로 결심함

questions.text: 이 이야기가 주는 교훈으로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: 도시 생활이 시골 생활보다 항상 더 좋다.
부유하지만 위험한 삶보다 소박하지만 평화로운 삶이 낫다.
맛있는 음식을 친구와 나눠 먹어야 한다.
questions.explanation: 시골쥐의 마지막 말 "I would rather eat my simple food in peace than have your fine food in fear"가 이야기의 핵심 교훈임

passages.title: How to Be a Good Friend
passages.text: What does it mean to be a good friend? It's not just about having someone to hang out with. A good friend is someone you can trust. They keep your secrets and are honest with you, even when the truth is hard to hear. A good friend is also a good listener. They listen to your problems without judging you. They celebrate your successes and support you when you fail. Friendship is a two-way street. It means you also have to be a good friend to others. You should be loyal and kind. Remember to make time for your friends and show them you care. A true friendship is a precious gift.

questions.text: What is the main topic of the passage?
choices: The best ways to have fun with friends
The qualities of a good friend
The problems that can happen in a friendship
How to make new friends at school
questions.explanation: 글 전체가 좋은 친구가 되기 위한 조건, 즉 '좋은 친구의 자질'에 대해 설명하고 있음

questions.text: According to the passage, which is NOT a quality of a good friend?
questions.explanation: 좋은 친구는 자신의 문제에 대해 잘 들어주는 사람("a good listener")이라고 했으므로, 자기 얘기만 하는 것은 좋은 친구의 자질이 아님

questions.text: 위 글에서 "Friendship is a two-way street"가 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: 좋은 친구가 되려면 나 자신도 좋은 친구가 되어주어야 한다.
questions.explanation: '우정은 쌍방향 길이다'라는 의미로, 상대방에게 좋은 친구가 되어주기를 바란다면 나 자신도 그래야 한다는 상호적인 관계를 의미함

passages.title: The Life of a Honeybee
passages.text: Honeybees are fascinating insects. They live together in large groups called colonies. Each bee in the colony has a specific job. The queen bee is the mother of all the other bees. Her only job is to lay eggs. The male bees are called drones. Their job is to mate with the queen. Most bees are female worker bees. They have many important jobs. They collect nectar and pollen from flowers, build the honeycomb, and defend the hive from enemies. They work very hard their whole lives. The honey they produce is not for us; it's their food for the winter. So next time you enjoy some sweet honey, remember the hard work of these amazing creatures.

questions.text: What is the passage mainly about?
choices: The different roles of bees in a colony
How to make honey at home
The life cycle of a flower
questions.explanation: 여왕벌, 수벌, 일벌 등 군집 내에서 각 꿀벌이 맡은 다른 역할에 대해 설명하고 있음

questions.text: What is the main job of the queen bee?
questions.explanation: 본문에 "Her only job is to lay eggs"라고 명시됨

questions.text: 위 글의 내용과 일치하지 않는 것은?
questions.explanation: 꿀은 겨울을 나기 위한 자신들의 식량("their food for the winter")이라고 했으므로, 인간을 위해 만든다는 것은 틀린 설명

passages.title: My Dream Job: A Webtoon Artist
passages.text: When I grow up, I want to be a webtoon artist. A webtoon is a type of digital comic that you read on your smartphone or computer. I love drawing and telling stories, so I think it's the perfect job for me. To become a good webtoon artist, I need to practice drawing every day. I also need to read a lot of books and watch movies to get new ideas for my stories. A successful webtoon needs interesting characters and an engaging plot. It's also important to learn how to use digital drawing tools. It's a very competitive field, but I am willing to work hard to achieve my dream. I hope one day people will enjoy reading my webtoons.

questions.text: What is the writer's dream job?
questions.explanation: 글의 첫 문장에 "I want to be a webtoon artist"라고 명시됨

questions.text: According to the passage, what is NOT needed to become a good webtoon artist?
questions.explanation: 그림 실력, 이야기 아이디어, 디지털 도구 사용법, 노력 등은 언급되었지만 노래 실력은 언급되지 않음

questions.text: 위 글을 통해 알 수 있는 글쓴이의 성격으로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: 남의 말을 잘 듣지 않고 고집이 세다.
questions.explanation: 자신의 꿈이 무엇인지 명확히 알고, 그것을 이루기 위해 무엇을 해야 하는지 구체적으로 계획하고 노력하려는 자세를 보임

passages.title: The Benefits of Laughter
passages.text: They say that "laughter is the best medicine," and it's true. Laughter has many positive effects on our body and mind. When you laugh, your body releases chemicals called endorphins. Endorphins are natural pain relievers and make you feel happy. Laughter also improves your immune system, which helps your body fight off disease. It can even be a good workout for your muscles! A good, hearty laugh can relax your whole body. Furthermore, laughter is a social activity. It connects us with other people and strengthens our relationships. So, try to find humor in your daily life. Watch a funny movie, share a joke with a friend, or just laugh at yourself. It's good for your health.

questions.text: What is the main idea of the passage?
choices: How to make other people laugh
questions.explanation: 웃음이 우리 몸과 마음에 미치는 여러 가지 긍정적인 효과에 대해 설명하고 있음

questions.text: According to the passage, what does laughter release in our body?
questions.explanation: 웃을 때 우리 몸은 엔도르핀이라는 화학 물질을 방출한다고("releases chemicals called endorphins") 나옴

questions.text: 위 글에 언급된 웃음의 효과가 아닌 것은?
questions.explanation: 고통 완화, 면역력 향상, 근육 이완, 관계 강화 등은 언급되었지만 기억력 향상은 언급되지 않음

passages.title: A Message in a Bottle
passages.text: One day, a young boy named Finn was walking along the beach. He loved collecting seashells and interesting stones. Suddenly, he saw a small glass bottle in the sand. Inside, there was a piece of rolled-up paper. His heart beat with excitement. He carefully opened the bottle and took out the paper. It was a letter, but the writing was faded and hard to read. It seemed very old. The letter was from a sailor named Arthur. He wrote that his ship was caught in a big storm in 1985. He didn't know if he would survive. He just wanted someone to know his story. Finn felt a mix of sadness and wonder. He decided to keep the letter as a treasure, a secret from the sea.

questions.text: What did Finn find on the beach?
choices: A bottle with a letter inside
questions.explanation: 해변에서 편지가 든 유리병을 발견했다고 나옴

questions.text: What was the letter about?
choices: A map to a hidden treasure
A message from a sailor in a storm
A list of things to do on a ship
questions.explanation: 1985년 폭풍우에 갇힌 선원이 쓴 편지였음

questions.text: 위 글의 내용과 일치하는 것을 고르시오.
choices: 병 속에 담긴 편지는 읽기 쉬웠다.
questions.explanation: 편지에 "his ship was caught in a big storm in 1985"라고 쓰여 있었음

passages.title: The School Play
passages.text: Our English class decided to perform a short play. We chose "The Tortoise and the Hare." I was chosen to play the role of the Tortoise. I was happy because the Tortoise is the hero of the story. My best friend, Minho, played the Hare. We practiced for weeks. I had to learn how to walk very slowly and speak with a confident voice. Minho was good at pretending to be fast and a little bit arrogant. On the day of the play, I was nervous, but I remembered all my lines. The audience laughed when the Hare took a nap, and they cheered when I crossed the finish line first. It was a great experience to work together with my classmates and create something fun for everyone.

questions.text: What is the passage mainly about?
choices: The story of "The Tortoise and the Hare"
The writer's experience in a school play
The friendship between the writer and Minho
How to become a good actor
questions.explanation: 학교 연극에 참여한 경험을 중심으로 글이 전개됨

questions.text: What role did the writer play?
questions.explanation: 본문에 "I was chosen to play the role of the Tortoise"라고 명시됨

questions.text: 위 글의 내용과 일치하지 않는 것은?
choices: 글쓴이는 거북이 역할을 맡은 것을 기뻐했다.
민호는 빠르고 거만한 산토끼 연기를 잘했다.
글쓴이는 연극 당일 대사를 모두 잊어버렸다.
관객들은 거북이가 결승선을 통과했을 때 환호했다.
questions.explanation: 본문에 "I remembered all my lines"라고 했으므로, 대사를 잊어버렸다는 것은 틀린 설명

passages.title: A Day Without a Smartphone
passages.text: My teacher gave us a strange homework assignment: to live for one whole day without our smartphones. I thought it would be impossible. My phone is my alarm clock, my camera, my music player, and my way to talk to friends. The next morning, I woke up late because I didn't have my phone's alarm. During the day, I felt anxious. I kept reaching for my phone in my pocket, but it wasn't there. I couldn't check social media or watch videos. But then, something interesting happened. I started to notice the world around me more. I talked to my family more during dinner. I even picked up a book that I had wanted to read for months. It was a difficult but valuable day. It taught me that I should control my phone, not let my phone control me.

questions.text: What was the writer's special homework?
choices: To write a report about smartphones
To spend a day without a smartphone
To create a new smartphone app
To teach his parents how to use a smartphone
questions.explanation: 선생님이 내준 숙제는 하루 동안 스마트폰 없이 지내는 것이었음

questions.text: How did the writer feel at the beginning of the day?
questions.explanation: 본문에 "I felt anxious"라고 명시되어 있으며, 주머니에서 계속 폰을 찾는 등 불안하고 불편한 모습을 보임

questions.text: 위 글을 통해 '스마트폰 없는 하루'가 글쓴이에게 준 긍정적인 변화가 아닌 것은?
choices: 주변 세상에 더 관심을 갖게 되었다.
questions.explanation: 스마트폰 알람이 없어서 늦잠을 잤다고("woke up late") 했으므로, 일찍 일어났다는 것은 틀린 설명

passages.title: The Northern Lights
passages.text: Have you ever heard of the Aurora Borealis, also known as the Northern Lights? It is a natural light display in the sky, usually seen in the high-latitude regions around the Arctic. The lights appear in many colors, from pale green and pink to shades of red, yellow, and blue. They dance across the sky in beautiful patterns. What causes this amazing phenomenon? It happens when electrically charged particles from the sun enter the Earth's atmosphere. For many ancient cultures, the Northern Lights were a source of mystery and legends. Some believed they were spirits of the dead. Today, many tourists travel to places like Alaska, Norway, and Iceland just to witness this spectacular show of nature.

questions.text: What is the main topic of the passage?
choices: An explanation of the Northern Lights
The different colors of the rainbow
questions.explanation: 오로라가 무엇인지, 원인, 관련된 전설, 관측 장소 등을 설명하고 있음

questions.text: Where can the Northern Lights usually be seen?
choices: In tropical regions near the equator
In big cities with a lot of lights
In high-latitude regions near the Arctic
questions.explanation: 본문에 "usually seen in the high-latitude regions around the Arctic"이라고 명시됨

questions.text: 위 글의 내용과 일치하는 것을 고르시오.
choices: 오로라는 태양에서 온 입자가 지구 대기와 만나 발생한다.
옛날 사람들은 오로라의 원인을 정확히 알고 있었다.
questions.explanation: 본문에 "It happens when electrically charged particles from the sun enter the Earth's atmosphere"라고 명시됨

passages.title: My First Pet, a Goldfish
passages.text: When I was six years old, I won a goldfish at a school fair. I named him Goldie. He was my very first pet. I was so excited. I put him in a small fishbowl in my room. Every day, I would feed him and watch him swim around. But I didn't know how to take care of a fish properly. I gave him too much food, and I didn't change the water often enough. One morning, I found Goldie floating at the top of the water. He was gone. I was heartbroken. My mom explained that it wasn't my fault and that taking care of a living creature is a big responsibility. That experience, though sad, taught me an important lesson about life and responsibility.

questions.text: How did the writer get his first pet?
choices: His parents bought it for him.
He found it in a river.
He won it at a school fair.
His friend gave it to him.
He bought it at a pet store.
questions.explanation: 학교 축제에서 금붕어를 탔다고("won a goldfish at a school fair") 나옴

questions.text: Why did the goldfish die?
choices: The writer didn't feed it at all.
The writer didn't take care of it properly.
questions.explanation: 너무 많은 먹이를 주고 물을 자주 갈아주지 않는 등 제대로 돌보지 못했다고("didn't take care of a fish properly") 나옴

questions.text: What lesson did the writer learn from this experience?
choices: Taking care of a pet is a big responsibility.
He should never have a pet again.
Crying does not help solve problems.
questions.explanation: 글의 마지막에 "taking care of a living creature is a big responsibility"라는 교훈을 배웠다고 명시됨

passages.title: The Power of a Simple "Thank You"
passages.text: How often do you say "thank you"? These two simple words are more powerful than you might think. Saying "thank you" is a way of showing gratitude. It tells someone that you appreciate what they have done for you. It can be for a big thing, like a birthday present, or a small thing, like someone holding a door open for you. When you thank someone, it makes them feel good. It makes them feel valued and respected. This can encourage them to be kind to others as well. Showing gratitude is also good for you. It helps you focus on the positive things in your life and can make you feel happier. So, don't forget to use this magic phrase. It costs nothing but can make a big difference.

questions.text: What is the main message of the passage?
choices: How to get more birthday presents
The different ways to open a door
Why people should be more positive
questions.explanation: 글 전체가 '감사합니다'라는 말이 왜 중요하고 어떤 힘을 가졌는지 설명하고 있음

questions.text: According to the passage, how does saying "thank you" affect the person who hears it?
choices: It makes them feel valued and respected.
questions.explanation: 본문에 "it makes them feel valued and respected"라고 명시됨

questions.text: 위 글에 따르면, "감사합니다"라고 말하는 것이 우리 자신에게 좋은 이유는 무엇인가?
choices: 삶의 긍정적인 면에 집중하게 하고 더 행복하게 만든다.
questions.explanation: 본문에 "It helps you focus on the positive things in your life and can make you feel happier"라고 명시됨

passages.title: A Mistake in a Test
passages.text: Last week, I had a big math test. I studied really hard for it. During the test, I thought I did well. But when I got my test paper back, I saw a big red "X" on the last question. I had made a simple calculation mistake. Because of that one mistake, I got a B instead of an A. I was so disappointed and angry with myself. I complained to my older sister. She listened and then said, "Everyone makes mistakes. The important thing is what you do next. Do you understand why you made the mistake? Can you learn from it?" Her words made me think. I realized that one test score doesn't define who I am. I decided to learn from my mistake and be more careful next time.

questions.text: Why was the writer disappointed with his math test?
choices: He didn't have enough time to finish.
He made a simple mistake and got a lower grade.
He forgot to write his name on the paper.
questions.explanation: 간단한 계산 실수를 해서 A 대신 B를 받았기 때문에 실망했다고 나옴

questions.text: What advice did the writer's sister give him?
choices: She told him to complain to the teacher.
She said math is not important.
She told him to learn from his mistake.
She offered to do his homework for him.
She told him to give up on math.
questions.explanation: 누나가 실수로부터 배우는 것이 중요하다고("learn from it") 조언함

questions.text: 이 글을 통해 글쓴이가 얻은 교훈으로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: 시험 점수가 그 사람의 모든 것을 결정한다.
실수로부터 배우고 다음을 준비하는 것이 중요하다.
누나에게 불평하는 것은 도움이 되지 않는다.
questions.explanation: 누나의 조언을 통해 실수에서 배우고 다음을 준비하는 것이 중요하다는 교훈을 얻음

passages.title: The Secret Life of Trees
passages.text: We see trees every day, but do we really know them? Trees are much more complex than they look. They have a secret life underground. Their roots spread out like a huge network. Through this network, trees can "talk" to each other. They can share water and nutrients with trees that are sick or in need. They can also send warning signals. For example, if one tree is attacked by harmful insects, it can release a chemical signal through its roots to warn its neighbors. The other trees then produce chemicals to defend themselves. This underground network is sometimes called the "Wood Wide Web." It shows that trees are not just individual plants, but a community that helps each other.

questions.text: What is the "Wood Wide Web"?
choices: The underground root network that connects trees
A book about the secret life of trees
A new kind of computer network
questions.explanation: 나무들을 연결하는 지하의 뿌리 네트워크를 "Wood Wide Web"이라고 부른다고 설명함

questions.text: How can trees "talk" to each other?
choices: They cannot talk to each other.
questions.explanation: 뿌리 네트워크를 통해 화학 신호를 보내 "대화"한다고 나옴

questions.text: 위 글의 내용과 일치하지 않는 것은?
choices: 나무는 뿌리를 통해 서로 영양분을 공유할 수 있다.
한 나무가 공격을 받으면 이웃 나무에게 경고를 보낼 수 있다.
나무는 서로 돕는 공동체를 이루고 있다.
나무는 오직 자기 자신만을 위해 살아간다.
questions.explanation: 나무들은 서로 영양분을 나누고 경고를 보내는 등 서로 돕는 공동체라고 했으므로, 자기 자신만을 위해 살아간다는 것은 틀린 설명

passages.title: My First Part-time Job
passages.text: When I turned 16, I decided to get my first part-time job. I wanted to earn my own money and gain some work experience. I got a job at a local bakery. My main duty was to pack bread and cookies into bags for customers. I also had to keep the display cases clean and tidy. The job was not easy. I had to stand for hours, and it was busy, especially on weekends. But I enjoyed the smell of fresh bread and the friendly atmosphere. My boss was kind and taught me a lot. I learned how to be more responsible and how to communicate with different people. The best part was getting my first paycheck. I felt so proud because I had earned it myself.

questions.text: Why did the writer want to get a part-time job?
choices: To learn how to bake bread
To earn money and gain experience
Because his parents told him to
questions.explanation: 스스로 돈을 벌고 직업 경험을 얻고 싶어서("earn my own money and gain some work experience") 일을 시작함

questions.text: What was the writer's main duty at the bakery?
choices: Packing bread and cookies for customers
questions.explanation: 주된 임무는 빵과 쿠키를 봉투에 포장하는 것이라고("pack bread and cookies into bags") 나옴

questions.text: 이 아르바이트 경험을 통해 글쓴이가 배운 점이 아닌 것은?
questions.explanation: 책임감, 소통 능력, 돈의 가치 등은 배웠지만, 빵을 굽는 기술을 배웠다는 내용은 언급되지 않음

passages.title: The Art of Origami
passages.text: Origami is the Japanese art of paper folding. "Ori" means "folding," and "kami" means "paper." Using a single sheet of square paper, you can create amazing shapes like animals, flowers, or boxes, without using scissors or glue. Origami is not just a fun craft; it is also very beneficial. It helps improve concentration because you have to follow the instructions carefully. It also develops hand-eye coordination and fine motor skills. Furthermore, it teaches patience and problem-solving. When your model doesn't look right, you have to figure out what went wrong. The most famous origami shape is the paper crane. There is a Japanese legend that if you fold one thousand paper cranes, your wish will come true.

questions.text: What is Origami?
choices: The Japanese art of paper folding
The Japanese art of flower arrangement
The Japanese art of sword fighting
questions.explanation: 글의 첫 문장에 "Origami is the Japanese art of paper folding"이라고 정의됨

questions.text: According to the passage, what is a benefit of doing origami?
choices: It teaches you how to use scissors.
questions.explanation: 본문에 "It helps improve concentration"이라고 명시됨

questions.text: 위 글의 내용과 일치하는 것을 고르시오.
choices: 종이접기를 할 때는 가위와 풀이 필수적이다.
종이접기는 인내심을 기르는 데 도움이 된다.
종이학 천 마리를 접으면 돈을 많이 벌게 된다는 전설이 있다.
종이접기는 주로 둥근 모양의 종이를 사용한다.
questions.explanation: 본문에 "it teaches patience"라고 명시됨

passages.title: A Solar Eclipse
passages.text: A solar eclipse is a rare and amazing astronomical event. It happens when the Moon passes directly between the Sun and Earth, and the Moon fully or partially blocks the Sun. This casts a shadow onto Earth. For a total solar eclipse, the sky becomes dark as if it were night. The stars come out, and the air gets cooler. It is a truly awesome sight. However, it is very dangerous to look directly at the sun during an eclipse. The sun's rays can permanently damage your eyes. You must use special eclipse glasses or a pinhole projector to watch it safely. The next time you hear about a solar eclipse happening, make sure you are prepared to view it safely.

questions.text: What causes a solar eclipse?
choices: The Earth passes between the Sun and the Moon.
The Moon passes between the Sun and the Earth.
A big cloud covers the Sun.
The Sun moves away from the Earth.
questions.explanation: 달이 태양과 지구 사이를 지나갈 때 일어난다고 설명됨

questions.text: Why is it dangerous to look directly at a solar eclipse?
choices: It can make you feel dizzy.
The sky is too dark to see.
The sun's rays can damage your eyes.
It can make you feel cold.
questions.explanation: 본문에 "The sun's rays can permanently damage your eyes"라고 명시됨

questions.text: 위 글에서 일식을 안전하게 관찰하는 방법으로 언급된 것은?
questions.explanation: 본문에 "use special eclipse glasses or a pinhole projector"라고 명시됨

passages.title: The School Newspaper Club
passages.text: I am a member of the school newspaper club. We publish a new edition of our newspaper, "The Eagle Times," every month. As a reporter, my job is to find interesting stories happening in our school. Last month, I wrote an article about the new recycling program. I interviewed the science teacher and some students. It was exciting to see my article in print. Our club has different roles. Some students are editors who check for grammar and spelling mistakes. Others are photographers who take pictures for the articles. We all work together as a team. Being in the newspaper club has improved my writing skills and taught me how to meet a deadline. It's a lot of work, but it's very rewarding.

questions.text: What is the writer's role in the newspaper club?
questions.explanation: 본문에 "As a reporter, my job is to find interesting stories"라고 명시됨

questions.text: According to the passage, what is NOT a benefit of being in the club?
choices: It teaches how to meet a deadline.
It helps make a lot of money.
questions.explanation: 글쓰기 능력 향상, 마감 준수, 보람, 팀워크 등은 언급되었지만 돈을 버는 것과는 관련이 없음

questions.text: 위 글의 내용과 일치하는 것을 고르시오.
choices: 글쓴이는 지난달에 학교 축제에 대한 기사를 썼다.
신문 동아리에는 기자, 편집자, 사진 기자 등 다양한 역할이 있다.
신문 동아리 활동은 일이 적고 매우 쉽다.
questions.explanation: 기자, 편집자, 사진 기자 등 다양한 역할이 있다고 설명함

passages.title: The Four Seasons
passages.text: Each season of the year has its own special beauty. Spring is a time of new beginnings. The weather gets warmer, flowers bloom, and birds return from the south. Summer is the warmest season. The days are long, and it's a perfect time for swimming and eating ice cream. Then comes autumn, or fall. The leaves on the trees change color to red, yellow, and orange. The air becomes cool and crisp. It's the season of harvest. Finally, winter arrives. It can be very cold, and sometimes it snows. It's a cozy time to drink hot chocolate and read books by the fire. Each season has its own unique charm, and I enjoy them all.

questions.text: What is the main purpose of the passage?
choices: To describe the beauty of each season
To give advice on what to wear in winter
To explain why leaves change color
questions.explanation: 봄, 여름, 가을, 겨울 각 계절의 특징과 아름다움을 묘사하고 있음

questions.text: According to the passage, in which season do flowers bloom?
questions.explanation: 본문에 "Spring is a time... flowers bloom"이라고 명시됨

questions.text: 위 글의 내용과 일치하지 않는 것은?
questions.explanation: 가을에는 나뭇잎이 빨강, 노랑, 주황색으로 변한다고 했으므로 초록색으로 변한다는 것은 틀린 설명

passages.title: A Public Library
passages.text: A public library is a wonderful place in our community. It's not just a building full of books. It's a place where you can learn, explore, and connect with others. The best part is that most of its services are free. You can borrow books, magazines, and even movies. If you need a quiet place to study, the library is perfect. Many libraries also offer free Wi-Fi and have computers for public use. They often host events like story time for children, book clubs for adults, and workshops on various topics. The librarians are always there to help you find what you need. A library is a valuable resource for everyone, no matter how old you are. It opens up a world of knowledge and imagination.

questions.text: What is the main idea of the passage?
choices: The many benefits and services of a public library
The rules you must follow in a library
The most popular books in the library
questions.explanation: 도서관이 책을 빌리는 곳 이상의 다양한 서비스와 혜택을 제공하는 장소임을 설명하고 있음

questions.text: Which service is NOT mentioned in the passage?
choices: Events like story time and workshops
questions.explanation: 책 대여, 공부 장소, 와이파이, 행사 등은 언급되었지만 커피나 간식을 파는 기능은 언급되지 않음

questions.text: 위 글에 따르면, 도서관이 우리에게 왜 가치 있는 장소인가?
choices: 최신 영화를 가장 먼저 볼 수 있어서
돈을 많이 벌 수 있는 기회를 제공해서
지식과 상상력의 세계를 열어주는 귀중한 자원이기 때문에
친구들과 자유롭게 떠들며 놀 수 있어서
questions.explanation: 글의 마지막에 "A library is a valuable resource... It opens up a world of knowledge and imagination"이라고 요약하고 있음

passages.title: The Story of Helen Keller
passages.text: Helen Keller was born in 1880. When she was a baby, she had a serious illness that left her both blind and deaf. She could not see or hear. She lived in a dark and silent world, and she was often angry and frustrated. When she was six, her parents hired a teacher named Anne Sullivan. Anne was very patient. She taught Helen how to communicate by spelling words into her hand. The first word Helen understood was "water." It was a breakthrough moment. From then on, Helen learned quickly. She learned to read Braille and even went to college. She became a famous writer and speaker, inspiring millions of people around the world. Her story teaches us that with courage and determination, we can overcome any obstacle.

questions.text: What happened to Helen Keller when she was a baby?
choices: She learned to read and write.
She became blind and deaf due to an illness.
She met her teacher, Anne Sullivan.
questions.explanation: 아기 때 심각한 병으로 인해 시력과 청력을 모두 잃었다고("left her both blind and deaf") 나옴

questions.text: Who was Anne Sullivan?
questions.explanation: 헬렌에게 세상을 가르쳐준 선생님 이었음

questions.text: 헬렌 켈러의 이야기가 우리에게 주는 교훈으로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: 용기와 굳은 결심이 있다면 어떤 어려움도 극복할 수 있다.
questions.explanation: 글의 마지막에 "with courage and determination, we can overcome any obstacle"라고 교훈을 명시하고 있음

passages.title: The Importance of Teamwork
passages.text: In a basketball game, one player, no matter how talented, cannot win the game alone. They need their teammates. They need to pass the ball, defend together, and communicate on the court. This is called teamwork. Teamwork is not just important in sports. It's essential in many parts of our lives, like in school projects or even in a family. When we work as a team, we can combine our different skills and ideas. Someone might be good at planning, while another person is good at drawing. By working together, we can achieve a goal that would be difficult to achieve alone. Teamwork also teaches us how to listen to others, compromise, and respect different opinions. It makes the final result much better.

questions.text: What is the main topic of the passage?
choices: The importance and benefits of teamwork
How to be a good leader
The best way to plan a project
questions.explanation: 농구 경기를 예시로 들어 팀워크의 중요성과 여러 장점을 설명하고 있음

questions.text: According to the passage, what can we do when we work as a team?
choices: We can ignore other people's opinions.
We can combine different skills and ideas.
We can finish the work faster without planning.
We can make one person do all the work.
questions.explanation: 본문에 "we can combine our different skills and ideas"라고 명시됨

questions.text: 위 글에서 언급된 팀워크의 장점이 아닌 것은?
choices: 혼자서는 어려운 목표를 달성할 수 있다.
다른 사람의 의견을 듣는 법을 배운다.
항상 내 의견을 주장할 수 있다.
다양한 기술과 아이디어를 합칠 수 있다.
questions.explanation: 팀워크는 다른 사람의 의견을 듣고 타협하는 법을 가르쳐준다고 했으므로, 항상 내 의견만 주장하는 것은 팀워크의 장점이 아님

passages.title: A Surprise Visitor
passages.text: One evening, while I was doing my homework, I heard a tapping sound on my window. I ignored it, thinking it was just the wind. But the tapping came again, a little louder this time. I felt a bit scared. I slowly walked to the window and pulled back the curtain. To my surprise, a small owl was sitting on the windowsill! It had big, round, yellow eyes and was looking right at me. I had never seen an owl so close before. I stood there for a moment, just watching it. The owl tilted its head and then flew away silently into the night. It was a magical and unexpected moment. I will never forget my little surprise visitor.

questions.text: What was the source of the tapping sound on the window?
questions.explanation: 커튼을 열어보니 창가에 작은 올빼미가 앉아 있었다고 나옴

questions.text: How did the writer feel when he first heard the sound?
questions.explanation: 본문에 "I felt a bit scared"라고 명시됨

questions.text: 위 글의 분위기로 가장 적절한 것은?
questions.explanation: 한밤중에 예기치 않은 올빼미의 방문은 마법 같고 신비로운 분위기를 자아냄

passages.title: The History of Chocolate
passages.text: Chocolate is one of the world's most popular foods, but where did it come from? The story of chocolate begins in ancient Central America with the Maya and Aztec people. They made a bitter, frothy drink from the beans of the cacao tree. They believed cacao was a gift from the gods. This drink was very different from the sweet chocolate we eat today. In the 1500s, Spanish explorers brought cacao beans back to Europe. The Europeans added sugar and milk to the drink, which made it much more popular. For a long time, chocolate was a luxury item only for the rich. It wasn't until the 19th century that solid chocolate bars were invented, making chocolate a treat that everyone could afford.

questions.text: What is the passage mainly about?
questions.explanation: 고대 중앙아메리카에서 시작되어 유럽을 거쳐 오늘날에 이르기까지 초콜릿의 변천 과정을 설명하고 있음

questions.text: How was the original chocolate drink made by the Maya and Aztec people?
choices: It was mixed with milk and sugar.
It was served cold with ice.
It was eaten as a bar.
questions.explanation: 마야와 아즈텍인들은 쓰고 거품이 많은 음료를 만들었다고 나옴

questions.text: 위 글의 내용과 일치하는 것을 고르시오.
choices: 고대 마야인들은 카카오를 신의 선물이라고 믿었다.
초콜릿은 처음부터 모든 사람이 즐길 수 있는 음식이었다.
오늘날 우리가 먹는 초콜릿과 옛날 초콜릿 음료는 맛이 똑같다.
questions.explanation: 본문에 "They believed cacao was a gift from the gods"라고 명시됨

passages.title: My Fear of Public Speaking
passages.text: I have a big fear of public speaking. Whenever I have to give a presentation in front of the class, my hands get sweaty, my heart beats like a drum, and my mind goes blank. Last week, I had to present my science project. I was so nervous. The night before, I practiced in front of the mirror many times. I also made cue cards with key points to help me remember. When it was my turn, I took a deep breath and started. At first, my voice was shaky. But as I talked, I started to feel more confident. I remembered my practice and used my cue cards. In the end, I finished my presentation without any big mistakes. My teacher and friends clapped. I was so relieved and proud of myself for facing my fear.

questions.text: What is the writer's biggest fear?
questions.explanation: 글의 첫 문장에 "I have a big fear of public speaking"이라고 명시됨

questions.text: What did the writer do to prepare for his presentation?
choices: He asked a friend to do it for him.
He practiced in front of a mirror and made cue cards.
He decided not to go to school.
He read a book about public speaking.
He drank a lot of water.
questions.explanation: 발표 전날 거울 앞에서 연습하고 대본 카드("cue cards")를 만들었다고 나옴

questions.text: 글쓴이가 발표를 마친 후 느꼈을 심경으로 가장 적절한 것은?
questions.explanation: 두려움을 이겨내고 발표를 성공적으로 마친 후 안도감과 자랑스러움을("relieved and proud") 느낌

passages.title: How Do Birds Fly?
passages.text: Have you ever watched a bird fly and wondered how it does that? Birds are masters of flight, and their bodies are perfectly designed for it. First, they have very light bones. Many of their bones are hollow, which makes their skeletons much lighter than those of mammals. Second, they have powerful flight muscles in their chest to flap their wings. Third, and most importantly, they have feathers. Feathers are light, strong, and flexible. They help birds to lift off the ground and steer in the air. The shape of a bird's wings is also important. It creates a difference in air pressure, which helps to lift the bird up. It's a perfect combination of lightness, power, and aerodynamic design.

questions.text: What is the main question this passage answers?
choices: Where do birds go in winter?
questions.explanation: 글 전체가 새가 어떻게 날 수 있는지 그 원리를 여러 가지 측면에서 설명하고 있음

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT a reason why birds can fly, according to the passage?
choices: Their wings have a special shape.
questions.explanation: 가벼운 뼈, 강력한 근육, 깃털, 날개 모양은 언급되었지만, 음식을 적게 먹는다는 내용은 언급되지 않음

questions.text: 위 글에서 새의 뼈가 가진 특징으로 언급된 것은 무엇인가?
questions.explanation: 본문에 "Many of their bones are hollow, which makes their skeletons much lighter"라고 명시됨

passages.title: A Letter to My Future Self
passages.text: Today in class, our teacher asked us to write a letter to our future selves. We had to write about our current life, our friends, our dreams, and our worries. We then put the letters in a time capsule, which we will open on our graduation day in three years. In my letter, I wrote about my dream of becoming a veterinarian because I love animals. I wrote about my best friend, Sora, and the funny things we do together. I also wrote about my worry that I'm not good enough at math. I asked my future self: "Did you get into a good university? Are you still friends with Sora? Did you get a puppy?" It was a strange but interesting experience. I wonder how much I will have changed in three years.

questions.text: What did the writer do in class?
choices: He wrote a letter to his teacher.
He wrote a letter to his best friend.
He wrote a letter to his future self.
He gave a presentation about his dream.
questions.explanation: 미래의 자신에게 편지를 쓰는("write a letter to our future selves") 활동을 함

questions.text: Which of the following did the writer NOT write about in his letter?
choices: His dream of becoming a veterinarian
questions.explanation: 수의사라는 꿈, 친구 소라, 수학에 대한 걱정 등은 썼지만, 좋아하는 음식에 대한 내용은 언급되지 않음

questions.text: When will the writer open the time capsule?
questions.explanation: 타임캡슐을 3년 후 졸업식 날("on our graduation day in three years") 열어볼 것이라고 나옴

passages.title: The World of Sign Language
passages.text: Have you ever seen people talking with their hands? They are likely using sign language. Sign language is a complete language that uses hand gestures, facial expressions, and body movements to communicate. It is not a universal language; different countries have their own sign languages, just like spoken languages. For example, American Sign Language (ASL) is different from British Sign Language (BSL). For many deaf and hard-of-hearing people, sign language is their primary way of communication. Learning sign language can be a wonderful experience. It allows you to communicate with a new community of people. It also helps you become more aware of the diverse ways people interact with the world.

questions.text: What is sign language?
choices: A secret code used by spies
A universal language that everyone knows
A language that uses hands, face, and body to communicate
A simple way to say "hello" and "goodbye"
A written language for deaf people
questions.explanation: 손짓, 얼굴 표정, 몸짓을 사용해 의사소통하는 완전한 언어라고 설명함

questions.text: According to the passage, which statement is TRUE?
choices: Sign language is the same all over the world.
Only deaf people can learn sign language.
American Sign Language and British Sign Language are identical.
Sign language uses only hand gestures.
Different countries have their own sign languages.
questions.explanation: 미국 수화와 영국 수화가 다른 것처럼, 나라마다 고유의 수화가 있다고("different countries have their own sign languages") 명시됨

questions.text: 위 글에서 수화를 배우는 것의 장점으로 언급되지 않은 것은?
choices: 새로운 공동체의 사람들과 의사소통할 수 있다.
사람들이 세상을 살아가는 다양한 방식에 대해 더 잘 알게 된다.
더 좋은 직업을 쉽게 구할 수 있다.
청각 장애를 가진 사람들과 소통하는 데 도움이 된다.
손과 얼굴 표정을 사용하는 새로운 언어를 배우게 된다.
questions.explanation: 새로운 공동체와 소통, 다양한 삶의 방식 이해 등은 언급되었지만, 직업을 구하기 쉽다는 내용은 없음

passages.title: A Camping Trip Gone Wrong
passages.text: My family loves camping. Last summer, we went to a new campsite in the mountains. We set up our tent and were excited for a night under the stars. However, the weather forecast was wrong. Suddenly, dark clouds gathered, and it started to rain heavily. The wind was so strong that our tent almost blew away. We were all huddled inside, listening to the thunder. The rain leaked into our tent, and our sleeping bags got wet. We couldn't make a campfire or cook our dinner. We had to eat cold sandwiches. It was not the fun camping trip we had expected. But looking back, it was a memorable adventure. It taught us to always be prepared for unexpected situations.

questions.text: What was the main problem during the camping trip?
choices: They forgot to bring their tent.
They got lost in the mountains.
questions.explanation: 일기예보가 틀리고 갑자기 날씨가 나빠져 비바람이 몰아쳤다고 나옴

questions.text: What did the family have for dinner?
choices: Hot dogs cooked over a campfire
Fish they caught from the river
questions.explanation: 모닥불을 피울 수 없어서 차가운 샌드위치를 먹었다고("had to eat cold sandwiches") 나옴

questions.text: 이 경험을 통해 글쓴이의 가족이 배운 교훈은 무엇인가?
choices: 예상치 못한 상황에 항상 대비해야 한다.
questions.explanation: 글의 마지막에 "it taught us to always be prepared for unexpected situations"라고 교훈을 명시함

passages.title: The Great Wall of China
passages.text: The Great Wall of China is one of the most impressive structures ever built by humans. It is not a single wall but a series of walls and fortifications. It stretches for thousands of kilometers across northern China. The main purpose of the wall was to protect China from invasions by nomadic tribes from the north. The construction of the wall started over 2,000 years ago and continued for centuries. Millions of soldiers and common people worked to build it. The wall was built using various materials, including stone, brick, and earth. Today, the Great Wall is a famous tourist attraction. It is a symbol of China's long history and the perseverance of its people.

questions.text: What was the main purpose of the Great Wall of China?
choices: To be a road for trade
To mark the center of the country
questions.explanation: 북쪽 유목민들의 침략으로부터 중국을 보호하는 것이 주된 목적이었다고("to protect China from invasions") 나옴

questions.text: Which statement about the Great Wall is NOT true?
choices: It is a single, continuous wall.
It stretches for thousands of kilometers.
It was built over many centuries.
Millions of people worked to build it.
It is now a famous tourist site.
questions.explanation: 하나의 단일한 벽이 아니라 여러 벽과 요새의 연속이라고("not a single wall but a series of walls") 했으므로, 단일한 벽이라는 설명은 틀림

questions.text: 위 글을 통해 만리장성이 상징하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
questions.explanation: 글의 마지막에 "a symbol of China's long history and the perseverance of its people"이라고 명시됨

passages.title: Why Do We Yawn?
passages.text: Everyone yawns. You might yawn when you are tired or bored. But have you ever wondered why we do it? For a long time, scientists believed that we yawn to get more oxygen into our blood. However, recent studies suggest a different reason. The main theory now is that yawning helps to cool down our brain. Your brain works best when it is at a certain temperature. When your brain gets too warm, a yawn can help bring cooler blood to it. This might explain why we yawn more when we are tired, as tiredness can raise brain temperature. It also explains why yawning is contagious. Seeing someone else yawn might be a signal to our brain that it's time to cool down too.

questions.text: What is the new theory about why we yawn?
choices: To show that we are bored
questions.explanation: 최근 이론은 하품이 뇌를 식히는 데 도움을 준다는 것이라고 설명함

questions.text: The word "contagious" in the passage means:
choices: something that is easily spread from one person to another.
something that is difficult to understand.
something that is good for your health.
something that happens only when you are tired.
questions.explanation: '전염성이 있는'이라는 의미로, 한 사람에게서 다른 사람에게 쉽게 퍼지는 것을 뜻함

questions.text: 위 글의 내용과 일치하는 것을 고르시오.
choices: 하품은 혈액 속 산소를 부족하게 만든다.
뇌가 너무 차가워질 때 하품을 한다.
하품이 전염되는 이유는 아직 아무도 모른다.
피곤할 때 뇌의 온도가 올라갈 수 있다.
하품의 원인에 대한 과학적 이론은 변한 적이 없다.
questions.explanation: 피곤할 때 뇌 온도가 올라갈 수 있고, 이것이 하품을 더 많이 하는 이유일 수 있다고 설명함

passages.title: A Visit to a Traditional Market
passages.text: Last weekend, I went to a traditional market with my grandmother. It was very different from the clean, quiet supermarket I usually go to. The market was crowded and noisy, but it was full of life and energy. The air was filled with the smells of fresh vegetables, fried chicken, and various spices. Vendors were shouting to attract customers. My grandmother seemed to know everyone. She greeted many of the shop owners. She taught me how to pick the freshest fish and how to bargain for a better price. We bought a lot of things and our hands were full. It was a fun experience. The traditional market is not just a place to shop; it's a place full of human connection and culture.

questions.text: How was the traditional market different from a supermarket?
choices: It was crowded, noisy, and full of energy.
It was cleaner and more modern.
questions.explanation: 시장은 붐비고 시끄러웠지만 활기와 에너지가 넘쳤다고 묘사됨

questions.text: What did the writer's grandmother teach him at the market?
choices: How to greet people in different languages
How to pick fresh fish and bargain
questions.explanation: 할머니가 신선한 생선을 고르는 법과 가격을 흥정하는 법을 가르쳐주었다고 나옴

questions.text: 위 글에서 글쓴이가 생각하는 재래시장의 가장 큰 특징은 무엇인가?
choices: 사람 사이의 정과 문화를 느낄 수 있는 장소라는 점
questions.explanation: 글의 마지막에 "a place full of human connection and culture"라고 결론 내리고 있음

passages.title: The Life of a Butterfly
passages.text: The life of a butterfly is a story of amazing transformation. This process is called metamorphosis. A butterfly's life has four stages. First, a female butterfly lays her eggs on a leaf or stem. This is the egg stage. Second, a larva, which we call a caterpillar, hatches from the egg. The caterpillar's main job is to eat and grow. It eats a lot and grows very quickly. Third, when the caterpillar is big enough, it forms a protective shell around itself called a chrysalis (or pupa). This is the pupa stage. Inside the chrysalis, an incredible change happens. Finally, a beautiful butterfly emerges from the chrysalis. It is ready to fly, drink nectar, and start the cycle all over again.

questions.text: What is the correct order of a butterfly's life stages?
questions.explanation: 알(egg) → 애벌레(larva) → 번데기(pupa) → 나비(butterfly) 순서로 설명됨

questions.text: What is the main job of a caterpillar?
questions.explanation: 애벌레의 주된 임무는 먹고 자라는 것이라고("main job is to eat and grow") 명시됨

questions.text: 위 글에서 '번데기' 단계를 영어로 무엇이라고 하는가?
questions.explanation: 번데기 단계는 "pupa stage"라고 하며, 번데기 자체는 "pupa" 또는 "chrysalis"라고 함

passages.title: The Lost Dog
passages.text: One afternoon, a little girl named Lily was playing in her yard when she saw a small dog. The dog looked lost and scared. It had a collar but no name tag. Lily felt sorry for the dog. She slowly approached it and gave it some water. The dog seemed friendly. Lily's mom helped her make some "Lost Dog" posters. They put the dog's picture on the poster and their phone number. They put the posters up all around their neighborhood. The next day, they got a call. It was from an elderly man who lived a few blocks away. He was so happy and relieved. His dog, Sparky, had run out of the house the day before. Lily was sad to say goodbye to Sparky, but she was happy that she could help him find his way home.

questions.text: What did Lily do to find the dog's owner?
choices: She took the dog to an animal shelter.
She made and put up "Lost Dog" posters.
She walked around the city with the dog.
She posted about the dog on the internet.
questions.explanation: 강아지 사진과 전화번호를 넣은 '주인 찾는 개' 포스터를 만들어 동네에 붙였다고 나옴

questions.text: Who was the owner of the lost dog?
choices: The poster didn't work, so they don't know.
questions.explanation: 몇 블록 떨어진 곳에 사는 할아버지("an elderly man")가 주인으로 밝혀짐

questions.text: How did Lily feel at the end of the story?
choices: Angry that she had to give the dog back
Sad to say goodbye, but happy to have helped
Happy to keep the dog forever
Disappointed that the owner was found
Worried that the dog would get lost again
questions.explanation: 스파키와 헤어지는 것은 슬펐지만, 집을 찾는 것을 도와줄 수 있어서 기뻤다고("sad to say goodbye... but she was happy that she could help") 나옴

passages.title: The Benefits of Reading Newspapers
passages.text: In this digital age, many people get their news from the internet or social media. However, reading a physical newspaper still has many benefits. First, newspapers are written by professional journalists and checked by editors, so the information is generally more reliable than what you find on social media. Second, reading a newspaper can improve your vocabulary and reading comprehension skills. You are exposed to a wide range of topics and writing styles. Third, it helps you understand the world better. You can learn about politics, economics, culture, and sports all in one place. It can make you a more informed citizen. So, try picking up a newspaper once in a while.

questions.text: What is the main point of the passage?
choices: The internet is the best source of news.
questions.explanation: 글 전체가 종이 신문을 읽는 것의 여러 가지 이점에 대해 설명하고 있음

questions.text: According to the passage, why is newspaper information often more reliable?
choices: It is written by famous people.
It is checked by professional journalists and editors.
questions.explanation: 전문 기자들이 쓰고 편집자들이 확인하기 때문에("written by professional journalists and checked by editors") 정보가 더 신뢰할 만하다고 함

questions.text: 위 글에서 신문을 읽는 것의 장점으로 언급되지 않은 것은?
choices: 더 많은 정보를 가진 시민이 되게 한다.
소셜 미디어보다 더 빨리 소식을 접할 수 있다.
신뢰할 만한 정보를 얻을 수 있다.
questions.explanation: 정보의 신뢰성, 어휘력 향상, 세상 이해 등은 언급되었지만, 소셜 미디어보다 빠르다는 내용은 없음

passages.title: The Mystery of the Bermuda Triangle
passages.text: The Bermuda Triangle, also known as the Devil's Triangle, is a region in the western part of the North Atlantic Ocean. It is famous for a mystery: many ships and airplanes have reportedly disappeared there under mysterious circumstances. Some people have proposed strange explanations. They blame aliens, giant sea monsters, or portals to another dimension. However, scientists and experts offer more logical explanations. They point out that this area has very bad weather, including many hurricanes. The ocean floor in this region is also very deep, making it hard to find any wreckage. Furthermore, many of the stories of disappearances have been exaggerated or are not accurate. While the mystery is fun to think about, the reality is likely less supernatural.

questions.text: What is the Bermuda Triangle famous for?
choices: The mysterious disappearances of ships and planes
questions.explanation: 많은 배와 비행기가 미스터리하게 사라진 것으로 유명하다고("famous for a mystery: many ships and airplanes have reportedly disappeared") 나옴

questions.text: Which of the following is a logical explanation for the disappearances?
choices: The ships and planes were invisible.
questions.explanation: 과학자들은 허리케인과 같은 악천후("bad weather, including many hurricanes")를 논리적인 설명으로 제시함

questions.text: 위 글에 따르면, 버뮤다 삼각지대에 대한 작가의 생각은 무엇인가?
choices: 외계인이나 괴물과 같은 초자연적인 현상이 원인일 것이다.
미스터리는 재미있지만, 실제 원인은 악천후 등 현실적인 이유일 가능성이 높다.
버뮤다 삼각지대는 세상에서 가장 위험한 곳이므로 가서는 안 된다.
사라진 배와 비행기에 대한 모든 이야기는 사실이다.
버뮤다 삼각지대의 미스터리는 영원히 풀리지 않을 것이다.
questions.explanation: 글의 마지막 부분에서 미스터리는 흥미롭지만, 현실은 초자연적이기보다는 악천후 등 현실적인 이유일 가능성이 높다고 서술함

passages.title: How to Make a Good First Impression
passages.text: They say you never get a second chance to make a first impression. A first impression is the feeling or opinion someone forms about you when they meet you for the first time.
